displayName: Full Stdlib Port of Axon's Context Graph into cnogo

decisions.decision: New scripts/context/ package parallel to scripts/memory/
decisions.rationale: Emphasizes 'context' over raw graph. Clean separation from memory engine. Follows existing package pattern.
Graph has different lifecycle than memory — can be rebuilt from source without affecting task tracking. Clean separation.

decisions.decision: OOP ContextGraph class with index/query/impact/context methods
decisions.rationale: Stateful graph object better matches the domain (graph holds repo state). Mirrors axon's pattern.

decisions.decision: Triple trigger: explicit CLI, auto on /review and /plan, PostCommit hook
decisions.rationale: CLI for manual control, auto for workflow convenience, hooks for freshness. All three cover different use cases.

decisions.decision: Incremental re-indexing from day one via file hash tracking
decisions.rationale: Scales to real codebases. Track content hashes per file, only re-parse changed files. Axon's get_indexed_files() pattern.

decisions.decision: Python ast module only (no tree-sitter in MVP)
decisions.rationale: Stdlib-only constraint. ast module is mature for Python. Multi-language support deferred to post-MVP via layered deps.

decisions.decision: MVP is core 6 phases: file walking, structure, Python parsing, imports, call tracing, heritage
decisions.rationale: Minimum for a useful call graph. Impact analysis, dead code, coupling, communities, flows are post-MVP phases.

decisions.decision: Port axon's GraphNode/GraphRelationship dataclasses and NodeLabel/RelType enums
decisions.rationale: Proven data model with 10 node types and 11 relationship types. Deterministic IDs (label:file:name).

decisions.decision: BFS from entry points (main, CLI dispatch, test_) through forward CALLS edges, creating Process nodes with STEP_IN_PROCESS edges at each depth
decisions.rationale: Answers 'how does execution reach this function?'. Reuses existing entry point heuristics from dead_code phase. Forward BFS mirrors impact analysis's reverse BFS pattern.

decisions.decision: Entry points detected via existing _is_entry_point() heuristic plus if-name-main guard detection in AST
decisions.rationale: dead_code.py already identifies main(), test_*, Test*, __init__.py symbols. Extend with explicit __name__=='__main__' block detection for script entry points.

decisions.decision: SQLite FTS5 virtual table over symbol names, signatures, and docstrings with BM25 ranking
decisions.rationale: FTS5 is built into stdlib sqlite3, no external deps. BM25 ranking gives relevance-sorted results. Enables fuzzy keyword search without exact symbol names.

decisions.decision: FTS table populated during index() pipeline, rebuilt on incremental reindex
decisions.rationale: Keeps search index in sync with graph. FTS5 supports content-sync via INSERT/DELETE, so incremental updates are efficient.
User chose porter over unicode61 default. Better recall for natural language in docstrings (e.g., 'parsing' matches 'parse'). Slightly fuzzier but appropriate for code search.

decisions.decision: Docstrings extracted via ast.get_docstring() and stored in nodes.content column
decisions.rationale: content column already exists in schema but is unpopulated. Docstrings are the primary natural language text for FTS search. Extracted during symbols phase.

decisions.decision: USES_TYPE edges persisted from ParseResult.type_refs via new types phase module
decisions.rationale: Type refs are already parsed by PythonParser but not stored as edges. Connecting symbols to their type references completes the type dependency graph.

decisions.decision: EXPORTS edges persisted from ParseResult.exports via new exports phase module
decisions.rationale: Exports are already parsed from __all__ but not linked in graph. EXPORTS edges connect files to their publicly exported symbols.

constraints: Python 3.10+ stdlib only — no pip dependencies
install.sh is the single distribution mechanism
Must not break existing memory engine or workflow commands
SQLite for storage (via stdlib sqlite3 module)
openQuestions: Should graph-search CLI support prefix/wildcard queries in addition to BM25 full-text?

research.summary: Graph-powered code intelligence engine. 10 node types, 11 relationship types, 12-phase ingestion pipeline. 8 of 12 phases are stdlib-portable.
